items.name: loopback-theme
items.title: Loopback theme
items.description: Feedback-status and severity design tokens (open, triaged, in_progress, fixed, verified, wontfix, p0-p3) as CSS variables, in shadcn's oklch contract with light and dark values. Adds to your theme rather than replacing it: every variable is --lb- prefixed, so your own palette is untouched.
items.author: Dikshant Joshi <https://github.com/joshidikshant>
items.docs: Pairs with the loopback-widget item. Use the tokens directly (background: var(--lb-verified)) or through Tailwind arbitrary values (bg-[var(--lb-verified)]). Status names match the Loopback MCP bus exactly, so a badge coloured by var(--lb-{status}) always agrees with the queue.

items.name: loopback-widget
items.title: Loopback capture widget
items.description: The embeddable feedback widget itself: a dependency-free vanilla-JS IIFE that renders a floating toolbar, lets a user pin feedback on any element, auto-captures failing requests with their response bodies, console trail and AI run metadata, and turns pins green when an agent verifies the fix. Framework-agnostic — it runs in a shadow root and never touches your styles.
items.author: Dikshant Joshi <https://github.com/joshidikshant>
items.docs:
Load it with your project slug and a running Loopback hub (loopback-mcp-server --http):

<script src="/loopback-widget.js" data-project="my-app" data-endpoint="http://127.0.0.1:7077"></script>

In Next.js, render it from the root layout with next/script (strategy="afterInteractive"). The widget owns its own theme and reads none of your CSS variables, so it looks the same on every site.
